words.educational: Pyramids are ancient structures built as tombs for pharaohs. The Great Pyramid of Giza is one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

words.hint: Large territory ruled by a single authority
words.educational: An empire is an extensive group of states or countries under a single supreme authority. Famous examples include Roman, British, and Mongol empires.

words.hint: Period between ancient and modern times
words.educational: The Medieval period (Middle Ages) lasted from the 5th to 15th century in Europe, characterized by feudalism, castles, and knights.

words.hint: Brave fighter especially in ancient times
words.educational: Warriors were skilled fighters in many ancient cultures. Examples include samurai in Japan, Vikings in Scandinavia, and Spartans in Greece.

words.hint: Territory under control of another country
words.educational: A colony is a territory controlled by a foreign power. European colonization greatly impacted the Americas, Africa, and Asia.

words.hint: Major change in government or society
words.educational: A revolution is a fundamental change in political power. Major examples include the French Revolution and American Revolution.

words.hint: Line of rulers from the same family
words.educational: A dynasty is a sequence of rulers from the same family. China had many dynasties including Ming, Qing, and Tang.
A treaty is a formally concluded agreement between states. They've been used throughout history to end wars and establish alliances.
Pharaohs were the rulers of ancient Egypt, considered living gods. Famous pharaohs include Tutankhamun, Ramesses II, and Cleopatra.

words.hint: Object made by humans in the past
words.educational: Artifacts are objects created by humans, studied by archaeologists to learn about past cultures, technologies, and daily life.
